title: Garlicky Japanese Miso Ramen with Tadka and Pink Hue
description: A quick, keto-friendly ramen that blends garlicky Japanese miso broth, a fragrant Indian-style tadka, and a pop of pink color for a vibrant, low-carb dinner.

ingredients.name: Red chili flakes (or one dried chili, crushed)

instructions: Heat a medium pot over medium heat and add 1 teaspoon sesame oil.
Add mustard seeds and cumin; let them sizzle for 20 seconds to release aroma.
Stir in minced garlic, ginger, and chili flakes; cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Pour in the vegetable broth and bring to a gentle boil.
Whisk in the miso paste and tamari, then stir in the pink food coloring until evenly dispersed.
Add the rinsed shirataki noodles and simmer for 3–4 minutes until heated through.
Taste and adjust seasoning with extra tamari or a pinch of salt if needed.
Serve the broth and noodles in bowls, topping with sliced green onions and nori strips.
